home.idle_calm: It's okay. I'm here with you.
home.morning_nudge_sub: A small start helps the day feel steadier.
home.morning_nudge_speak: Morning check-in. Let's pick a mission.

onboarding.title: Hi! What is your child's name?

parent_onboarding.screen1_sub: Buddy helps children do small real-world things — move, tidy up, breathe. Not as a chore. As a game.
parent_onboarding.screen1_note: Validated by real kids. One called it a game after an hour of play.
parent_onboarding.screen2_body: Show Buddy to your child. Tap him together. Let your child pick the first mission themselves.
parent_onboarding.screen2_note: Your presence at the start is all that's needed. After that, they'll manage on their own.
parent_onboarding.screen3_body: When a child breathes slowly and rhythmically, their nervous system shifts from anxiety mode to calm mode. Not a metaphor — it's physiology.
parent_onboarding.screen3_try: Try it now — follow the circle
parent_onboarding.screen3_science: The longer exhale is the active ingredient — it directly signals the nervous system to slow down. The pattern was adapted and tested with a real child and adjusted until it worked.

demo.complete_msg: Want to try a real mission?

missionPick.title: Pick a mission
missionPick.encore_sub: Tomorrow Buddy will bring new missions.
missionPick.hint: Hold a card to hear it

rewards.title: Your rewards
rewards.hint: Tap a reward to get it

morning.ready_speak: Great. You are ready for the day.

breathing.title: Breathe together
breathing.subtitle: Two minutes of calm breathing with Buddy
breathing.pet_hint: Watch my breath and try to pet me when I exhale if you want.

progress.label_lt5: You are starting. That is great.

milestone.twenty: Twenty stars. You grow every day.
milestone.hundred: One hundred stars. A real star!

11.title: Ask mom or dad how to help
11.subtitle: A small task

15.title: Reach up high
15.subtitle: Hands as high as you can

dailySuggestions.10: Reach up. As high as you can.

settings.title: Parent settings
settings.snapshot_privacy: Stored only on this device. Screenshot-friendly for parent feedback.
settings.progress_empty: No missions completed yet. Stats will appear here.
settings.pin_label: PIN for rewards and Parent zone
settings.pin_sublabel: Protect rewards and Parent zone access
settings.pin_mission_sublabel: Require parent PIN before stars are added
settings.pin_change_sublabel: PIN is set for rewards and Parent zone
settings.pin_delete_msg: Reward protection will be turned off
settings.missions_parent_hint: Manage missions in the Parent zone
settings.infinity_loop_sub: A small set of missions each day, refreshed in the morning
settings.missions_per_day_sub: How many missions appear on the pick screen
settings.bonus_mission_sub: Offer one extra when all are done
settings.nudge_sub: Suggestion card on the home screen
settings.skip_sub: Buddy changes after {{count}} skips in a row
settings.rtl_sex_sub: Used only in RTL languages for natural voice phrasing
settings.facts_soon_sub: Buddy shares small facts and tells bear jokes during missions
settings.facts_hint_body: Facts appear during an active mission screen after a mission is picked.
settings.reset_progress_sub: Stars, missions and rewards will be cleared
settings.schedule_now_sub: Show what is happening now and next on the home screen
settings.schedule_home_card_sub: Show gentle day context on the home screen
settings.morning_reminder_sub: Show a gentle morning reminder about missions
settings.feedback_body: RealoKids is still growing. Tell us what would make it work better for your family.
settings.feedback_button: Tell us what RealoKids should do next
settings.feedback_email_body: Hi, I wanted to share this about RealoKids:
settings.about_realo_sub: Open the parent guide again: what RealoKids does, how to start together, and why breathing helps.
settings.credits_body: RealoKids (and music recording) was made by a father of an autistic child. Built with care. Not with a team.
settings.reset_full_msg: Stars, missions and history will be deleted. Settings are kept.
settings.mission_delete_msg: This mission will be deleted permanently
settings.reward_delete_msg: This reward will be deleted permanently

tiny_facts.f1: Your body has over 600 muscles. Standing on one leg uses most of them.
tiny_facts.f2: Touching your toes stretches your spine. It grows a tiny bit every night.
tiny_facts.f3: Jumping sends signals to your brain that say: wake up and feel good.
tiny_facts.f4: Your brain is 75% water. A glass of water helps it think faster.
tiny_facts.f5: One minute without a screen gives your eyes a real rest.
tiny_facts.f7: Squeezing your fists for three seconds tells your body to calm down.
tiny_facts.f8: Two minutes away from a screen helps your brain remember things better.
tiny_facts.f9: Tidying one corner makes the whole room feel different. Really.
tiny_facts.f10: A hug releases a chemical called oxytocin. It makes both people feel safer.
tiny_facts.f11: Asking for help is one of the bravest things a person can do.
tiny_facts.f12: A tidy space helps a calm mind. Even a small tidy counts.
tiny_facts.f13: Shaking your hands for ten seconds wakes up the nerves in your fingers.
tiny_facts.f14: Walking slowly and feeling the floor helps your brain know where you are.
tiny_facts.f15: Stretching tall makes you breathe deeper without even trying.
tiny_facts.f16: Cold water on your face sends a signal to your heart to slow down.
tiny_facts.f17: Three slow breaths can change how your whole body feels in under a minute.
tiny_facts.f18: Drinking water twice a day keeps your brain working at full speed.
tiny_facts.f19: A made bed is the first win of the day. Everything feels easier after.
tiny_facts.f20: Clearing your plate is a small act of care for the people around you.
tiny_facts.f21: Folded clothes stay cleaner. Your future self will thank you.
tiny_facts.f22: Saying thank you makes the other person feel seen. That matters a lot.
tiny_facts.f23: A smile uses fewer muscles than a frown. And it's contagious.
tiny_facts.f24: Sharing something good before bed helps your brain remember the day kindly.
tiny_facts.f25: One minute of silence gives your brain a chance to sort itself out.
tiny_facts.f26: Drawing activates a different part of your brain than words do.
tiny_facts.f27: Looking out the window rests your eyes and your thoughts at the same time.
tiny_facts.breathing_nature: Next time you are outside, try putting your attention on your breath for just a moment. Nature makes it easier.

tiny_jokes.j1: Why did the little bear sit in the shade? He didn't want to become a gummy bear.
tiny_jokes.j2: What do bears put on their toast? Honey — always honey.
tiny_jokes.j3: What is a bear's favorite kind of story? One with a pawsitive ending.
tiny_jokes.j4: Why was the teddy bear never hungry? He was already stuffed.
tiny_jokes.j5: What do you call a bear who loves quiet time? A hiber-napper.
tiny_jokes.j6: Why did the bear bring a backpack? For his beary important things.
tiny_jokes.j7: What do bears do when they are happy? They give big bear hugs.
tiny_jokes.j8: Why did the bear do so well in school? Because he knew all the bear-sics.
tiny_jokes.j9: What do you call a wet bear? A drizzly bear.
tiny_jokes.j10: Why don't bears use phones? Because they always drop the call when they hibernate.
tiny_jokes.j11: What do you call a clumsy bear? A fumble-bear.
tiny_jokes.j12: Why did the bear bring an umbrella? Just in case of a little drizzle.
